title: Dunes, Doom and Coronation: TEG 8 Decided at Oitavos
title_candidates: Dunes, Doom and Coronation: TEG 8 Decided at Oitavos
Williams Holds, Mullin Pounces: The Atlantic Verdict
Mullin's Jacket, Williams's Cup: Oitavos Settles It
theme: On a new links by the Atlantic, Gregg Williams sees out the Trophy while David Mullin snatches the Green Jacket from Jon Baker as early as the 2nd — and survives a quadruple at the 11th to keep it.
opening_hook: Open on Oitavos Dunes itself — TEG's first visit, second time on the Lisbon Coast — then to the verdict: two trophies handed out, one held, one taken.
foreshadow: Mullin's lead-grab at the 2nd that he then had to defend all afternoon
Mullin's quadruple at the 11th — the wobble that nearly handed it back to Jon Baker
Williams's quad at the 5th and the cold stretch through 5-8 that opened a door he later slammed shut on the back nine

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: WINNER. Williams arrived five clear and left twelve clear — but it wasn't serene. A quadruple at the 5th and an 11-shot bleed through holes 5-8 trimmed the cushion; a four-hole burst of 12 Stableford points across 12-15 (par-bogey-par-par) sealed the Trophy. Jon Baker, runner-up overnight, faded to third behind a rallying Alex Baker.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: WINNER. Mullin began the round two gross shots behind Jon Baker and took the outright lead with a bogey at the 2nd. A quadruple-bogey 8 at the 11th threatened to undo everything; he answered with a birdie at the 13th and another at the 16th to stop the bleeding twice over, finishing six clear of Baker.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: COLLECTED. Neumann began the round 22 clear at the bottom and finished 11 clear — Henry Meller's catastrophic front nine briefly raised hope, but quads at the 5th and 17th confirmed the Spoon's destination.

players.arc: Survived a quadruple at the 5th and an 11-shot bleed through 5-8, then put the Trophy to bed with 12 Stableford points across holes 12-15 (par-bogey-par-par).
Seized the Green Jacket outright with a bogey at the 2nd, weathered a quadruple-bogey 8 at the 11th, and twice stopped the bleeding with birdies at the 13th and 16th to win the gross title by six.
Overnight Jacket leader who lost the gross lead as early as the 2nd hole and could not reel Mullin back in, slipping to second on Gross and third on Stableford.
Climbed past Jon Baker into Trophy runner-up despite a quadruple at the 15th and a closing quintuple-bogey 9 at the 18th — a final-hole horror that didn't change the colour of his medal.
Endured the round of the week in the worst sense: 22 shots bled across holes 1-6, including a 10 at the 4th and a 9 at the 5th, then another nine-shot haemorrhage at 16-18 to close.
Quadruples at the 5th and 17th made certain of the Wooden Spoon, the 22-shot overnight cushion at the bottom shrinking only because Meller obliged.

venue_notes: Oitavos Dunes — TEG's first visit, Arthur Hills's Atlantic links at Cascais, and the second time the tour has rolled through the Lisbon Coast. A proper links finale: dunes, exposure, and a scorecard full of big numbers to prove it.
